extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT attacked solo 12km out on the Tolfè sector descent, time-trialed away from a select group including Bettiol/Formolo/Schachman on white gravel.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT attacked on Tolfè descent not climb, proving downhill acceleration on gravel neutralizes pure climbers' advantage.
Extreme heat eliminated traditional climber advantage—gap to 10th was 3:8 suggesting attrition favored diesel engines over pure watts.
VAN AVERMAET rode defensively watching others work, suggesting former winners prioritize podium over chasing longshot victory.
FULLSANG/FORMOLO burned matches early on San Martino then faded, indicating premature positioning before final sectors wastes legs.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won at 25 after two consecutive runner-up finishes, showing rapid gravel adaptation despite 2019 injury layoff
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: defending champion couldn't respond to downhill acceleration despite being present in decisive group
rider_signals.SCHACHMAN Max: neutral: made final selection but gapped when Van Aert attacked, suggesting strong endurance but lacking explosive gravel power
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: rode passively despite experience, suggesting age/form decline prevents offensive racing even on favorable terrain
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: negative: animated early on San Martino but cracked in finale, revealing Italian aggression backfires in extreme heat
